behavior_summary: U8P_FIRESHLD implements behavior for a Fire Shield item. Its primary visible behavior is to present the text 'fire shield ' when inspected (U8P_FIRESHLD::look event).

When the item is damaged (gotHit event) it ignores null attackers, determines attacker direction (uses NPC facing for NPC attackers or computes direction-to-attacker otherwise), maps that direction into two small byte parameters, plays a fixed impact sound, and—if the item's type/flags match expected values—allocates/prepares a resource and launches a hurl/throw process (Item::hurl) to fling either the item or a spawned object toward the attacker.

The gotHit handler contains a retry/cleanup loop that waits until a related process type (Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x0203, attackerId)) appears; if no process exists it calls a cleanup/free (FREE::2A56) and retries before finally starting Item::hurl.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIRESHLD::look (Event 0): set process info/exclusion state for the item, call Item::bark(self, "fire shield ") to show the label, free the string resources, then imply/suspend/return.

Direction resolution: if Item::isNpc(attackerId) is true then call Npc::getDir(attackerRef) else call Item::getDirToItem(self, attackerId). Store resolved direction (BP-02) and map via a branch table into two byte parameters (BP-04, BP-06) that drive the hurl.
Play SFX (playSFX(0x0064, 0x24)) unconditionally on hit before spawn checks.

Type/flag gating: call Item::getType(self) and test against constant/mask (observed constants 0x0105 and mask 0x60). Only when those checks pass does the script perform allocation (FREE::1A96) and proceed to spawn/hurl logic.

Spawn preparation includes METHOD::0080 and a loop that calls Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x0203, attackerId); if the count is zero it calls FREE::2A56 and retries until a process exists, then starts process Item::hurl with computed parameters and attackerId, then imply/suspend/return.
open_questions: Exact semantics of BP-04 and BP-06: they are mapped from direction and are likely velocity/trajectory or sprite/animation parameters for the hurl, but their precise meaning is inferred.
Precise role of FREE::1A96 and METHOD::0080: they appear to allocate/configure a spawnable resource or object but implementation details are not present.
Meaning of the Item::getType tests (constants 0x0105 and mask 0x60): what exact item flags/types are being tested and which item variants are allowed to spawn/hurl.
What specific object is created/launched by the allocation/Item::hurl sequence (the item itself, a missile, an effect?) is inferred but not confirmed.
Why Kernel::getNumProcesses is used as a gate (what process type 0x0203 represents) and whether the wait loop has side effects on gameplay timing.

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESHLD::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the item's 'U8P_FIRESHLD::look' (examine) event: set processing state and emit the human-facing label 'fire shield ' via Item::bark, then suspend/return.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (player/actor inspects or 'looks' at the Fire Shield item or enters its U8P_FIRESHLD::look trigger region).

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHLD::look
function_map.role: Examine/description handler that displays the item's label and sets item/process info state.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHLD::gotHit
function_map.role: Damage-response handler: compute attacker direction, play SFX, conditionally allocate/configure and start an Item::hurl process to throw/spawn toward attacker.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/display an item-centered text string to a viewer (used by U8P_FIRESHLD::look).

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Test whether an item id refers to an NPC (used to choose direction resolution).

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Return an NPC's facing direction (used as direction if attacker is an NPC).

function_map.name: Item::getDirToItem
function_map.role: Compute direction from this item to a target item/actor id (used if attacker is not an NPC and later in spawn setup).

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Return item type/flags for gating spawn/hurl behavior.

function_map.name: process Item::hurl
function_map.role: Process invoked to hurl/throw an item/spawned object using computed direction/params.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHLD::playSFX
function_map.role: Play a sound effect for hit/impact feedback.

function_map.name: FREE::1A96
function_map.role: Inferred allocator/creator used to allocate or prepare a resource/object for hurling.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Inferred helper to finalize spawn/configuration after allocation and direction determination.

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Query process counts (used as a gate to ensure a process of type 0x0203 for the attacker exists before launching hurl).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Inferred cleanup/free routine called when Kernel::getNumProcesses returns zero during retries.

external_calls.purpose: Display the item label 'fire shield ' when the player inspects the item.
Decide whether to use NPC facing or compute direction-to-item.
If attacker is an NPC, obtain that NPC's facing direction to determine throw direction.
If attacker is not an NPC (or for later spawn setup), compute direction from this item to attackerId.
Play a hit/impact sound effect (observed args 0x0064, 0x24) when the item is hit.
Read item type/flags to decide whether to spawn/launch an object on hit (gating condition).
Allocate or create a resource/object used for the subsequent hurl/spawn operation.
Finalize configuration/position relative to direction/self for the spawned object (inferred).
Wait/check for presence of processes of type 0x0203 owned by attackerId before proceeding; used as a gate/coordination point.
Cleanup/free called when Kernel::getNumProcesses indicates no required process exists (part of retry loop).
Start the hurl process with parameters derived from direction mapping and attackerId to throw/spawn toward the attacker.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_FIRESHLD implements behavior for a Fire Shield item. Its primary visible behavior is to present the text 'fire shield ' when inspected (U8P_FIRESHLD::look event).
